title: Some Factors Affecting Onset of Dendritic Morphology in Sodium Electrodeposition
authors: Yuanman Ma, Bairav S. Vishnugopi, Partha P. Mukherjee, Judith A. Jeevarajan and Rohan Akolkar
abstract: Sodium (Na) metal is a promising earth-abundant alternative to lithium for electrochemical energy storage; however, dendrite formation during charging of Na metal batteries remains a major hurdle. The solid electrolyte interphase (SEI), which forms on the Na metal surface, significantly influences the onset of Na dendrites. Here, we present optical microscopy observations of Na dendrite formation as a function of electrode preparation, applied current density, and choice of the organic electrolyte (diglyme vs carbonate). Observations suggest the critical role of the SEI in limiting Na+ transpo…
